Thinking, Fast and Slow|Daniel Kahneman.description: The foundational work on cognitive biases and dual-process theory.

The Color of Law|Richard Rothstein.description: How government policy created residential segregation in America.

Sapiens|Yuval Noah Harari.description: A sweeping history of humankind from the Stone Age to Silicon Valley.

Homo Deus|Yuval Noah Harari.description: Where humanity is headed next — from Homo sapiens to technological gods.

The Guns of August|Barbara Tuchman.description: The definitive account of the first month of World War I and the failures of diplomacy.

Capital in the Twenty-First Century|Thom.description: The landmark study of wealth inequality and its deep historical roots.

Evicted|Matthew Desmond.description: Pulitzer-winning account of poverty, housing, and eviction in America.

The Power Broker|Robert Caro.description: The epic biography of Robert Moses and the making of modern New York.

The Alignment Problem|Brian Christian.description: How machine learning reflects and amplifies human values — and where it goes wrong.

Superintelligence|Nick Bostrom.description: The canonical argument for why advanced AI poses existential risk.

The Structure of Scientific Revolutions|.description: The book that gave us 'paradigm shift' — how science actually changes.

Manufacturing Consent|Noam Chomsky.description: The propaganda model of mass media and how institutional filters shape the news.

The Origins of Totalitarianism|Hannah Ar.description: Arendt's analysis of how totalitarian movements arise from loneliness and terror.

On Tyranny|Timothy Snyder.description: Twenty lessons from the twentieth century on resisting authoritarianism.

Why Nations Fail|Daron Acemoglu.description: How political and economic institutions determine why some countries thrive and others don't.

The Righteous Mind|Jonathan Haidt.description: Why good people are divided by politics and religion — moral psychology explained.

Bowling Alone|Robert Putnam.description: The collapse and revival of American community — why social capital matters.

The Wealth of Nations|Adam Smith.description: The foundational text of modern economics and free market theory.

Meditations|Marcus Aurelius.description: The private journal of a Roman emperor — Stoic philosophy at its most personal.

The Art of War|Sun Tzu.description: The ancient Chinese treatise on strategy, still applied in business and conflict.

Brave New World|Aldous Huxley.description: Huxley's dystopia of pleasure, conformity, and engineered happiness.

1984|George Orwell.description: The definitive novel of surveillance, propaganda, and totalitarian control.

The Prince|Niccolò Machiavelli.description: The original handbook on political power — ruthlessly practical, endlessly debated.

A Theory of Justice|John Rawls.description: The most influential work of political philosophy in the twentieth century.

The Second Machine Age|Erik Brynjolfsson.description: How digital technology is transforming the economy, work, and prosperity.

The Road to Serfdom|Friedrich Hayek.description: Hayek's warning that central planning leads inevitably to authoritarianism.

Destined for War|Graham Allison.description: Can America and China escape Thucydides's Trap? History says it's hard.

The Clash of Civilizations|Samuel Huntin.description: The controversial thesis that cultural identity drives post-Cold War conflict.

On China|Henry Kissinger.description: Kissinger's insider account of Chinese strategic thinking from Mao to the present.

The Rise and Fall of the Great Powers|Pa.description: How economic strength and military overstretch determine the fate of empires.

Prisoners of Geography|Tim Marshall.description: How physical geography shapes geopolitics — why maps explain more than ideology.

The New Jim Crow|Michelle Alexander.description: Mass incarceration as the modern successor to Jim Crow racial control.

Between the World and Me|Ta-Nehisi Coate.description: Coates's searing letter to his son about being Black in America.

Caste|Isabel Wilkerson.description: America's hidden caste system — connecting race in America to caste worldwide.

How Democracies Die|Steven Levitsky.description: How elected leaders can gradually subvert democracy from within.

The Fourth Turning|William Strauss.description: The cyclical theory of American history — crisis, renewal, and generational patterns.

Amusing Ourselves to Death|Neil Postman.description: How television transformed public discourse from substance to entertainment.

The Master Algorithm|Pedro Domingos.description: The quest for the universal learning algorithm that will reshape civilization.

Life 3.0|Max Tegmark.description: What it means to be human in the age of artificial intelligence.

Human Compatible|Stuart Russell.description: How to build AI that is provably beneficial — from one of the field's founders.

The Black Swan|Nassim Nicholas Taleb.description: Why highly improbable events dominate history and how we systematically underestimate them.

Antifragile|Nassim Nicholas Taleb.description: Things that gain from disorder — a framework for thriving in uncertainty.

Nudge|Richard Thaler.description: How small design choices in how options are presented can dramatically improve decisions.

Factfulness|Hans Rosling.description: Ten instincts that distort our worldview — and why the world is better than you think.

The Innovator's Dilemma|Clayton Christen.description: Why great companies fail when disruptive technologies emerge.

Zero to One|Peter Thiel.description: Thiel's contrarian guide to building startups that create something genuinely new.

The Lean Startup|Eric Ries.description: Build-measure-learn — the methodology that changed how startups operate.

Atomic Habits|James Clear.description: Small changes, remarkable results — the science of habit formation.

Deep Work|Cal Newport.description: Rules for focused success in a distracted world.

The Age of AI|Henry Kissinger.description: How artificial intelligence will reshape security, politics, and human identity.

Gödel, Escher, Bach|Douglas Hofstadter.description: A Pulitzer-winning exploration of consciousness, formal systems, and the strange loops connecting math, art, and music.

The Beginning of Infinity|David Deutsch.description: A physicist's argument that explanations are the key to progress — and that progress is infinite.

The Age of Surveillance Capitalism|Shosh.description: How tech companies turned human experience into raw material for prediction and control.

The Circle|Dave Eggers.description: A novel about a powerful tech company that demands total transparency — and the cost of giving it.

Weapons of Math Destruction|Cathy O'Neil.description: How big data algorithms reinforce inequality and threaten democracy.

The Shock Doctrine|Naomi Klein.description: How crises are exploited to push through radical free-market policies.

Technopoly|Neil Postman.description: How technology became a totalitarian force that surrenders culture to its own imperatives.

Atlas of AI|Kate Crawford.description: How artificial intelligence is shaped by politics, labor, and the environment — not just code.

Automating Inequality|Virginia Eubanks.description: How high-tech tools profile, police, and punish the poor.

Race After Technology|Ruha Benjamin.description: How social inequality is embedded in digital design and the 'New Jim Code.'

The Wretched of the Earth|Frantz Fanon.description: Fanon's landmark analysis of colonialism, violence, and national liberation.

Orientalism|Edward Said.description: How the West constructed 'the Orient' as exotic other — and used that construction to justify domination.

The Fire Next Time|James Baldwin.description: Baldwin's searing essays on race, religion, and identity in America.

Darkness at Noon|Arthur Koestler.description: A political prisoner in a totalitarian state confronts the logic of revolution that devours its own.

The Peloponnesian War|Thucydides.description: The original work of political realism — Athens versus Sparta, and why democracies go to war.

The Diary of a Young Girl|Anne Frank.description: The intimate journal of a Jewish girl hiding from the Nazis — humanity's most personal Holocaust testimony.

Debt|David Graeber.description: A 5,000-year history of debt that upends everything you thought you knew about money and markets.

Thinking in Systems|Donella Meadows.description: A primer on systems thinking — how to see the interconnections that drive complex behavior.

The Narrow Corridor|Daron Acemoglu.description: How the constant struggle between state and society creates — or destroys — liberty.

The Tyranny of Merit|Michael Sandel.description: Why meritocracy breeds resentment and what a more humane politics might look like.

The Book of Why|Judea Pearl.description: The new science of cause and effect — why correlation is not causation and what to do about it.

Too Big to Fail|Andrew Ross Sorkin.description: The definitive inside account of the 2008 financial crisis and the people who caused it.

The Big Short|Michael Lewis.description: How a handful of investors saw the housing bubble and bet against Wall Street.

A Beautiful Mind|Sylvia Nasar.description: The biography of John Nash — mathematical genius, Nobel laureate, and his struggle with schizophrenia.

The Design of Everyday Things|Don Norman.description: Why some designs work and others don't — the psychology of good design.

Clean Code|Robert Martin.description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship — writing code that humans can read and maintain.

Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approa.description: The standard textbook on AI — comprehensive, authoritative, and used in universities worldwide.

All the President's Men|Bob Woodward.description: Woodward and Bernstein's account of uncovering Watergate — investigative journalism at its finest.

The Prize|Daniel Yergin.description: The epic history of oil, money, and power — from the first wells to the Gulf War.

The End of Alchemy|Mervyn King.description: A former central banker explains why the global financial system remains dangerously fragile.

The Great War for Civilisation|Robert Fi.description: Fisk's magisterial account of the Middle East's wars from the perspective of those who lived through them.
